what_it_does: A standalone HTML preview card rendering the full 9-step type scale as a table: each row names a role (Display, H1-H4, Body, Body sm, Caption, Eyebrow), shows a live sample at its size/weight, and lists the px/weight on the right.
observations: Carries a @dsCard annotation: group='Brand', name='Type scale', subtitle='9-step ramp from Display → Eyebrow', viewport='700x380'.
Display through H3 use the display font; H4 down switch to body font (.x.body). Eyebrow (.eb) is uppercase, bronze accent, tracked.
Each sample's font-size/weight is set inline per row; Body sample reuses brand copy 'The mathematics of operation.'
standouts: Encodes the font-family switch point: display font for Display-H3, body font from H4 down.
Defines a distinct Eyebrow style (uppercase, bronze, tracked) as a named scale step.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard gallery convention and var()-token discipline for fonts/colours/borders.
Per-row sizes/weights are inline literals (illustrative scale values), not references to size tokens.
notes: The 9-step type-scale reference card; shows every role's live sample, size/weight, the display→body font switch at H4, and the bronze Eyebrow style.
questions: Are these 9 steps codified as named size/weight tokens elsewhere, or only enumerated visually here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard names it the 'Type scale' 9-step ramp and that is exactly what renders.
intention_signals: Intended as the single eye-reference for the whole type hierarchy, so each role's size/weight/font and the family-switch point can be recognised at a glance.
novelty: Making the font-family switch (display→body at H4) and the Eyebrow's distinct bronze/uppercase treatment explicit within one ramp.
contribution: Contributes the consolidated 9-step type scale with per-role size/weight, the display-to-body font boundary, and the named Eyebrow style.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML @dsCard preview rendering the design system's 9-step type scale (Display through Eyebrow) with live samples, px/weight values, and the display-to-body font switch.
intention_for_embedding: To give a single eye-recognisable reference for the entire type hierarchy, each role's size/weight/font, and where the display font yields to body.
